member.name: M:EduApi.Auth.TokenService.CreateAccessToken(System.String,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Builds the access token. The client (services.dart) decodes the JWT payload client-side and reads: role (string OR array), groupId, schoolYear, exp. `unitIds` is a NEW comma-separated claim (only set for students) listing the UnitIds they're subscribed to (StudentUnitSubscription), so UnitsController can filter "GET Units" down to only what the student is actually subscribed to, instead of every unit in their school year.

member.name: M:EduApi.Common.ClaimsExtensions.GetStaffTenantId(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal)
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: the effective tenant id embedded in staff (Teacher/Assistant/ AssistantAdmin) JWTs as the "tenantId" claim (= TenantOwnerId ?? own Id, computed once at login/refresh). Students do NOT carry this claim — their tenant comes from the X-TenantId header instead (see ITenantContext).

member.name: M:EduApi.Common.ClaimsExtensions.GetUnitIds(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal)
member.summary: Student-only: the UnitIds from their StudentUnitSubscription rows, snapshotted into the "unitIds" claim at login/refresh (comma-separated). Empty list if the claim is absent (student not subscribed to anything, or a non-student token). NOTE: this is a snapshot — if a teacher subscribes/unsubscribes this student to a unit while their access token is still valid (up to Jwt:AccessTokenMinutes), they won't see the change until they refresh or log in again. Same tradeoff already accepted for groupId/schoolYear.

member.name: T:EduApi.Common.ITenantContext
member.summary:
TENANT LAYER ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── The Flutter app treats each Teacher as a separate "tenant". A student picks a teacher on SelectTeacherPage, stores that teacher's id locally as `tenantId`, and sends it on every subsequent request as the `X-TenantId` header (only for role == "student" — see AuthService._buildHeaders in main.dart). Teacher/Assistant/AssistantAdmin accounts never send that header; they always act on their own tenant, which is embedded directly in their JWT as the "tenantId" claim at login time (see AuthController + TokenService).
This service is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for "which tenant is this request for", consumed by AppDbContext's global query filters so every controller gets automatic data isolation for free — no per-controller tenant-filtering code needed.

member.name: P:EduApi.Common.ITenantContext.CurrentTenantId
member.summary: The resolved tenant (Teacher.Id) for the current request, or null if it cannot be resolved (no authenticated user, or a student request missing the X-TenantId header). When null, tenant-scoped global query filters will match zero rows — the safe default.

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.AppVersionsController
member.summary: Route: api/AppVersions GET AppVersions/latest (called with no auth requirement on app startup)

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.AttendanceController
member.summary: Route: api/Attendance POST Attendance?lectureId=.. POST Attendance/bulk?lectureId=.. The "encodedStudentId" is the payload embedded in each student's QR code (in this reconstruction: their numeric student id).

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.AuthController
member.summary: Route: api/Auth (matches Flutter: "$baseUrl/Auth/login", "$baseUrl/Auth/refresh")

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.CodesController
member.summary:
Route: api/Codes GET Codes/codeables?schoolYear=.. -> units (with nested lessons/lectures) + standalone lectures eligible for a code GET Codes?schoolYear=.. GET Codes/{codeId} POST Codes/{codeId}/delete -- now blocked once a code has been used POST Codes body: { schoolYear, unitIds, lectureIds }
Reconciled against the real client ("create Code .dart") — several things were mismatched before this pass and are now fixed to match the client exactly (see inline notes at each spot): 1) GetCodeables: client reads data['lectures'], not data['standaloneLectures']; and expects unit['lessons'][]['lectures'][] nested trees, not flat {id,name}. 2) Generate: client only treats HTTP 200 as success (`res.statusCode == 200`), not 201. 3) ToDto: client's list item reads `isRedeemed` (not `isUsed`) and `unlocks` (a list, only its .length is used); the details sheet reads `redeemedBy` (object), `units`/`lectures` (arrays of {name} objects, not raw id lists), and `redeemedAt` (timestamp string) — none of which existed before. 4) Delete: now returns 409 once a code IsUsed, per your request — deleting a redeemed code was allowed before with no restriction at all.

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.GroupsController
member.summary: Route: api/Groups GET Groups?schoolYear=.. POST Groups body: { schoolYear, name } POST Groups/delete?groupId=..

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.NotebooksController
member.summary: Route: api/Notebooks GET Notebooks?schoolYear=.. -> list, includes aggregated "paid" and "createdAt" POST Notebooks GET Notebooks/{id} -> details, "groups"/"units" hydrated with names GET Notebooks/{id}/payments -> each payment includes "student" + "totalPaid"

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.StudentsController
member.summary: Route: api/Students. This is the largest surface in the app; see class-level method XML docs for the exact endpoint each action reconstructs.

member.name: T:EduApi.Controllers.CreateTeacherRequest
member.summary: Route: api/Teachers. GET Teachers (client call: getWithAuth("Teachers", "student")) POST Teachers -- was missing entirely; nothing in the original app could create a Teacher account, only DbSeeder could. POST Teachers/edit -- was missing entirely; the only way to set Subject/ ImageUrl (both read directly by SelectTeacherPage's _TeacherCard, with NO null-check on imageUrl).

member.name: M:EduApi.Controllers.CreateTeacherRequest.#ctor(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Route: api/Teachers. GET Teachers (client call: getWithAuth("Teachers", "student")) POST Teachers -- was missing entirely; nothing in the original app could create a Teacher account, only DbSeeder could. POST Teachers/edit -- was missing entirely; the only way to set Subject/ ImageUrl (both read directly by SelectTeacherPage's _TeacherCard, with NO null-check on imageUrl).

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.Roles
member.summary: Roles as embedded in the JWT "role" claim. The Flutter client accepts either a single string role, or a list of roles where it prioritizes: AssistantAdmin > Teacher > Student > Assistant.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Teacher.Role
member.summary: Roles assigned to this account (Teacher / AssistantAdmin / Assistant).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Teacher.TenantOwnerId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: the "owning" tenant this account acts on behalf of. - null => this account IS a tenant root (a real Teacher who owns their own data). - set => this account is staff (Assistant / AssistantAdmin) working *for* another teacher's tenant; all their actions are scoped to TenantOwnerId's data. Effective tenant id = TenantOwnerId ?? Id. Computed once at login/refresh and embedded in the JWT as the "tenantId" claim (see TokenService/AuthController).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Teacher.Subject
member.summary: Shown on the student's SelectTeacherPage card. Nullable because staff accounts (Assistant/AssistantAdmin) never appear on that page — only tenant-root teachers (TenantOwnerId == null) do.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Teacher.ImageUrl
member.summary: Same as above: SelectTeacherPage does `Image.network(teacher['imageUrl'])` with NO null-check, so a tenant-root teacher without one WILL crash that screen client-side. Always send a non-null value for root teachers.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Group.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this group belongs to.

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.StateHistoryEntry
member.summary: History log for suspend/reactivate/cancel/delete actions on a student.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Unit.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this unit belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Lesson.LessonIndex
member.summary: Position of the lesson within the unit (used for lookups instead of Id).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Lesson.ImageUrl
member.summary: Mandatory cover image for the lesson (enforced in UnitsController.AddLesson).

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.StudentLectureUnlock
member.summary: Grants a student access to one standalone (no-unit) Lecture, created when a Code carrying that LectureId is redeemed (see StudentsController.RedeemCode). Mirrors StudentUnitSubscription, but for standalone lectures instead of units — without this, a noUnitOnly lecture from LecturesController.ByGroup had no gate at all and was visible to every student in the group regardless of any code.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Lecture.GroupIdsCsv
member.summary: Groups (as a joined string of ids) that this lecture is visible to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Lecture.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this lecture belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Material.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this material belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Notebook.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this notebook belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Code.UsedAt
member.summary: When the code was redeemed. The Flutter details sheet reads this as `data['redeemedAt']` to show "تم الاستخدام بنجاح" with a date — was missing entirely before (only IsUsed/UsedByStudentId existed).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Code.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this code belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Notification.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this notification belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Quiz.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this quiz belongs to.

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.CenterQuizResult
member.summary: Manually recorded center (offline) quiz/homework results, entered by a teacher via "Students/quiz-results/center/add" and "Students/homework-results/add".

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.Assignment
member.summary: Homework/assignment ("الواجب") set by a teacher for one or more groups, covering one or more units. Unlike Quiz (single unit, timed), an Assignment has no duration — just a Deadline after which no submission is accepted, and the correct answers are only revealed once that Deadline has fully passed (never immediately after a student submits).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.Assignment.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this assignment belongs to.

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.AssignmentSubmission
member.summary: A student's single submission of an Assignment.

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.BankQuestion
member.summary: A single reusable question in the teacher's "question bank" ("بنك الأسئلة"), always scoped to one specific Lesson and tagged with a Difficulty. Students later build their own timed practice quiz ("BankAttempt") by picking unit(s)/lesson(s) + difficulty + how many questions + a duration.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankQuestion.Difficulty
member.summary: "Easy" | "Medium" | "Hard".

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankQuestion.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this bank question belongs to.

member.name: T:EduApi.Models.BankAttempt
member.summary: A student-generated timed practice quiz pulled from the question bank. Behaves like a Quiz: the Deadline is computed at creation time (StartedAt + DurationMinutes), and the correction is shown immediately after submitting (or once the deadline passes), unlike Assignment.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankAttempt.TeacherId
member.summary: TENANT LAYER: which teacher (tenant) this attempt's bank belongs to.

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankAttempt.LessonIdsCsv
member.summary: Empty means "all lessons within the selected units".

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankAttempt.Difficulty
member.summary: "Easy" | "Medium" | "Hard" | null (any difficulty).

member.name: P:EduApi.Models.BankAttemptQuestion.ShuffledChoicesCsv
member.summary: Snapshot of this MCQ's choices in a randomized order, fixed at attempt creation time so re-loading the same attempt doesn't reshuffle the options underneath the student's already-selected answer. Empty for non-MCQ question types.

member.name: T:EduApi.Services.FileStorageService
member.summary:
Educational/demo implementation: stores files under wwwroot/uploads/{tenantId}/{subFolder}/ and serves them back as static files. Swap for S3/Azure Blob/Google Drive in production without changing any controller code — only this class needs to change.
TENANT ISOLATION ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Every file is namespaced by the current request's tenant (see ITenantContext / TenantContext.cs — same source of truth the EF Core global query filters use). This keeps each teacher's uploaded materials, unit images, teacher photos, and quiz-question images physically separated on disk, so: - There's no risk of one tenant's files colliding/overwriting another's even if two tenants upload a file with the exact same name. - Bulk operations (backup, export, delete) for a single tenant are a single folder copy/delete instead of a filtered scan. - If tenant data ever needs to be purged (GDPR-style request, account closure), `wwwroot/uploads/{tenantId}` can be deleted wholesale.
If the tenant cannot be resolved (which should not happen for any authenticated upload endpoint today — all of them are staff-only actions with a tenant embedded in the JWT), we fail loudly instead of silently writing to a shared/ambiguous folder, since that would be a silent tenant-isolation bug rather than a legitimate use case.

member.name: M:EduApi.Services.FileStorageService.TryExtractRelativePath(System.String)
member.summary: Recovers the on-disk relative path ("uploads/{tenantId}/{subFolder}/{file}") from a full URL we previously generated in SaveAsync. Returns null for anything that doesn't look like one of our own "/uploads/..." URLs.

member.name: M:EduApi.Services.FileStorageService.SanitizeSegment(System.String)
member.summary: Strips anything that isn't a plain folder-name character, blocking path traversal.

member.name: M:EduApi.Services.Interfaces.IFileStorageService.SaveAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.IFormFile,System.String)
member.summary: Saves an uploaded file to disk under the CURRENT request's tenant folder (uploads/{tenantId}/{subFolder}/...) and returns a publicly reachable URL. Tenant is resolved automatically from ITenantContext — callers never pass it.

member.name: M:EduApi.Services.Interfaces.IFileStorageService.DeleteAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Deletes a previously-uploaded file given the URL that SaveAsync returned for it (exactly as stored in the database). Safe to call with null/empty/ external URLs — those are simply ignored rather than throwing, since a failed cleanup of an old file should never block or fail the request that's replacing it.

member.name: T:EduApi.Services.R2FileStorageService
member.summary:
Cloudflare R2 implementation of IFileStorageService. R2 exposes an S3-compatible API, so we use AWSSDK.S3 pointed at R2's endpoint instead of AWS — no AWS account involved anywhere in this class.
TENANT ISOLATION (same policy as the local-disk implementation): Every object key is namespaced as "{tenantId}/{subFolder}/{guid}{ext}" so tenants can never collide or overwrite each other's files, and a whole tenant's files can be listed/deleted by prefix if ever needed.
SWAPPING: this class fully implements IFileStorageService, so it's a drop-in replacement for FileStorageService — only the DI registration in Program.cs needs to change (already done below). No controller changes.

member.name: M:EduApi.Services.R2FileStorageService.TryExtractKey(System.String)
member.summary: Recovers the R2 object key ("{tenantId}/{subFolder}/{guid}.ext") from a full public URL we previously generated in SaveAsync. Returns null for anything that isn't one of our own PublicBaseUrl URLs, so we never try to delete an externally-hosted image (e.g. one set via a raw ImageUrl field instead of a file upload).

member.name: T:EduApi.Services.R2Options
member.summary: Bound from the "R2" section in appsettings.json / environment variables. See README section "Cloudflare R2 setup" for where each value comes from.

member.name: P:EduApi.Services.R2Options.AccountId
member.summary: Your Cloudflare account ID (dashboard URL or R2 landing page). Used to build the S3 endpoint.

member.name: P:EduApi.Services.R2Options.AccessKey
member.summary: R2 API token's Access Key ID (R2 -> Manage API Tokens -> Create API Token).

member.name: P:EduApi.Services.R2Options.SecretKey
member.summary: R2 API token's Secret Access Key (shown ONCE at creation time — store it in a secret manager, not in source control).

member.name: P:EduApi.Services.R2Options.BucketName
member.summary: The R2 bucket name that stores all tenant uploads.

member.name: P:EduApi.Services.R2Options.PublicBaseUrl
member.summary: Public base URL for serving objects back to clients — either the bucket's r2.dev dev subdomain, or your own custom domain connected to the bucket (recommended for production). No trailing slash.

member.name: T:Program
member.summary: Auto-generated public partial Program class for top-level statement apps.
